items.description: Run a diffusion restyle on every frame of a clip, one frame at a time, and the still images look great. Then you play them back and the whole thing boils. items.title: Access Is Not Agency
items.description:
Your agent can read Slack. It can search email. It can query the CRM, open GitHub issues, check billing, browse docs, edit a spreadsheet, draft a customer reply, and call three internal APIs.

Everyone in the room calls it powerful. That is the first mistake.

items.title: Why My Polymarket Bot Watches Chainlink, Not Binance - The Oracle Gap That Changes Every Close Trade
items.description:
For the first few weeks I was running my bot, I was comparing Polymarket prices against Binance spot. Seemed obvious. BTC/USD is BTC/USD, right?

Wrong. Cost me real money before I figured out why.

items.description:

items.title: I let AI write this post. Then I deleted everything that made it sound like AI.
items.description:
A language model wrote this post. I directed it, but I didn't rewrite a single sentence by hand. Then I ran the draft through a small ruleset called Alchemy. If the writing below doesn't trip your "this is AI" alarm, that's the entire point, and I'll show you the machinery.

items.title: Browser Security Model: The Defensive Walls Every Hacker Knows (And Every Developer Should Too)
items.description:
"To defend a system, you must first think like the attacker."

I'll tell you this: the browser is one of the most hostile execution environments ever built. Every tab you open is potentially running untrusted code inches away from your banking session.
